nativeRange.habitat: Reef-associated; generally found in deep lagoons and seaward reefs at approximately 10–120 m depth in tropical waters between 30°N and 33°S

decorPreferences: live rock with numerous hiding places

diet.notes: An opportunistic omnivore. Has been documented in the wild feeding on benthic algae and small invertebrates (zooplankton, small crustaceans, polychaetes). In captivity, generally accepts a variety of meaty preparations (mysid shrimp, chopped marine seafoods, frozen preparations) supplemented with marine algae. Small frequent feedings several times daily are commonly advised, particularly during acclimation.

summary: A Pacific reef butterflyfish with a white body crosshatched by black-edged scales and an orange-yellow posterior. Generally considered intermediate-care and not reef-safe; best suited to large fish-only or FOWLR systems with ample swimming space.
careNotes:
The Mertensii Butterflyfish (Chaetodon mertensii) is a Pacific reef species belonging to the 'crowned' butterflyfish group. It is generally regarded as one of the more manageable Chaetodon species in captivity, though it still requires a well-established system and attentive feeding during acclimation.

Reef-safety: aquarium trade and reference sources consistently flag the species as not reef-safe. It has been documented nibbling on tubeworms, clams, large- and small-polyp stony corals, soft corals, and anemones. A fish-only or FOWLR setup is generally advised; experienced keepers occasionally report mixed success in reef systems, but this is the exception rather than the rule.

Conspecific behaviour: ReefApp notes the species 'flourishes better without other members of the same species,' and LiveAquaria adds that multiple butterflyfish can coexist if introduced simultaneously. The safer husbandry stance is to keep a single specimen unless tank volume and aquascape allow simultaneous introduction of multiple peaceful butterflyfishes.

Cross-source synthesis: Adult size varied between sources (LiveAquaria 6 in / ~15 cm; FishBase 12.5 cm TL; ReefApp 13 cm; Wikipedia 12.5 cm) — typical aquarium adults reach around 12–13 cm, so 10–13 cm has been used here. Temperature ranged from 22–28°C across sources (LiveAquaria 72–78°F / 22.2–25.6°C; FishBase preferred 23.7–28.4°C; ReefApp ~26°C); averaged to 24–27°C. Tank minimum volume showed a wide spread: LiveAquaria recommends 120 gal (~454 L) and ReefApp recommends 300 L; given the species' need for ample swimming space and the convention of biasing toward larger sizing for active mid-sized butterflyfish, a 500 L minimum has been used. No reliable lifespan figures were located across the sources consulted.

additional.name: FishBase
additional.notes: Cross-checked taxonomy (Family Chaetodontidae, Order Acanthuriformes), maximum length (12.5 cm TL), preferred temperature range (23.7–28.4°C, mean 26.8°C), depth range (0–120 m), and native distribution (Ryukyu and Philippines to Lord Howe, Rapa, and Tuamotus). Used in averaging the temperature range.

additional.name: ReefApp Encyclopedia
additional.notes: Cross-checked adult size (13 cm), minimum tank volume (300 L), reef-safety (not reef-safe; predation on tubeworms, clams, LPS, SPS), temperament (mostly peaceful, prefers to be the only conspecific), and care difficulty (hardy / 'suitable with care'). Used in tank-size disagreement averaging — final figure was biased toward the larger LiveAquaria value.

additional.name: Wikipedia
additional.notes: Used for taxonomy confirmation, additional common names (Atoll Butterflyfish, Yellowback Butterflyfish, Merten's Butterflyfish), wild diet (algae and small benthic invertebrates), and habitat depth (10–120 m, deep lagoons and seaward reefs).
